question_title: Will the interest in “helicopter” change between 2026-02-03 and 2026-02-14 according to Google Trends?
summary: Google Trends interest in "helicopter" on Feb 3 (60) is at the 18th percentile of recent history. Strong mean-reversion signal: all 7 cases where value was ≤60 showed increases 11 days later. Day-of-week effect (Saturday vs Tuesday) adds further upward pressure. Base rate for 11-day increases is 56%. "Doesn't change" is rare at ~5% historically. The evidence collectively favors "Increases" as the most likely outcome.

factors.description: Current value (60) at 18th percentile - strong mean reversion signal. All 7 historical cases where value ≤60 showed increase 11 days later.
Day-of-week effect: Feb 14 is Saturday (avg 73.4) vs Feb 3 Tuesday (avg 64.1), ~9 points higher on average.
Base rate for 11-day changes: 56% increase, 39% decrease, 5% unchanged - slightly favors increase even without conditioning.
Recent downtrend (80→57→60) could indicate a structural shift rather than temporary dip, moderating the increase signal.
Unknown resolution criteria - 'change' definition could use thresholds, averages, or point comparisons, introducing interpretation uncertainty.

**Key analysis:**

1. **Current value is at a low point**: Feb 3 value of 60 is at the 18th percentile of the last 3 months (mean 68.2). This is a strong mean-reversion signal — in all 7 historical cases where the value was ≤60, it increased 11 days later.

2. **Day-of-week effect**: Feb 3 is a Tuesday (avg 64.1), Feb 14 is a Saturday (avg 73.4). Saturdays consistently show higher search interest for "helicopter."

3. **Base rates**: Over 11-day windows, increases occurred 56% of the time, decreases 39%, no change 5%.

4. **"Doesn't change" is rare**: Exact matches over 11-day windows occur only ~5% of the time in this data.

5. **No major news catalyst** found for February 2026 that would dramatically spike or suppress interest.**Forecast: Increases 65% / Doesn't change 10% / Decreases 25%**

The dominant signal is mean reversion from an unusually low starting point. The Feb 3 value of 60 is at the 18th percentile of recent data, and historically every comparable low point recovered within 11 days. The Saturday vs Tuesday day-of-week effect further favors an increase. I assign modest probability to decreases (25%) to account for data noise, possible structural shifts, and unknown resolution criteria.
